claim: Document-specific vision models (not generic vision models) are essential for accurate document processing - they must understand spatial layouts, preserve structure across multiple pages, and handle complex tables and mixed content types
actionable: When selecting models for document extraction, use document-native vision models trained specifically for document understanding rather than generic vision models, as they excel at localizing content and understanding spatial relationships
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | David Park: Impact of Agentic AI in Financial Services on Document Extraction

claim: Agentic document extraction platforms should use datacentric approaches - continuously improving via feedback loops as the system processes more documents and encounters new data sources and patterns
actionable: Build document extraction systems with feedback loops so they improve continuously as you process more documents - capture user corrections and new document patterns to refine the model
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | David Park: Impact of Agentic AI in Financial Services on Document Extraction

claim: For compliance-heavy document workflows (like KYC/AML), implement schema-driven extraction where each document type has its own extraction schema - this ensures you extract only relevant fields and maintain traceability to source documents
actionable: When extracting data from compliance documents, define separate schemas for each document type (e.g., paystubs, bank statements, IDs) with field-level source grounding so auditors can trace every extracted value back to the original
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | David Park: Impact of Agentic AI in Financial Services on Document Extraction

claim: A real-world KYC automation case study achieved 70%+ reduction in manual document review by integrating agentic document extraction into existing RPA systems for banks processing thousands of analyst-heavy reviews globally
actionable: When evaluating document extraction systems for compliance workflows, look for evidence of 70%+ reduction in manual review time and integration capability with existing RPA/workflow systems
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | David Park: Impact of Agentic AI in Financial Services on Document Extraction

claim: Vision-grounded extraction provides cell-level references in tables, allowing auditors to verify exactly which cell the extracted value came from - this is essential for regulatory compliance and preventing LLM hallucination concerns
actionable: Implement vision grounding in document extraction pipelines so each extracted field includes coordinates or cell references showing precisely where the value came from, enabling full auditability
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | David Park: Impact of Agentic AI in Financial Services on Document Extraction

claim: Classification and splitting agents should work together - first classify pages by document type, then apply the appropriate extraction schema - this is more reliable than trying to extract from mixed document types at once
actionable: In multi-document processing, first implement a classification agent to identify document types, then split documents, then apply document-specific extraction schemas rather than a single catch-all extraction
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | David Park: Impact of Agentic AI in Financial Services on Document Extraction
